title: bunium alpinum subsp. corydalinum
intro: Bunium alpinum subsp. corydalinum, also known as alpine bunnyparsley, is a subspecies of the Bunium alpinum plant, a type of umbelliferous plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its carminative, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of alpine bunnyparsley include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and lower blood pressure. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture, to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
